Two SSRIs, fluoxetine and escitalopram, have been approved by the FDA for the treatment of depression in adolescents. Other SSRIs such as sertraline, NDRIs such as bupropion, and SNRIs such as venlafaxine are often used off-label for this purpose but have not been approved by the FDA for use in adolescents. Esketamine is a ketamine nasal spray used in conjunction with an oral antidepressant for treatment-resistant depression in adults.

A venous hum is an innocent heart murmur that is caused by vein vibration. It is commonly heard in children and may appear between 3 and 6 years of age. It is typically audible only when the child is upright, and changes with head movement. In a child with a venous hum, reassurance of the parents is appropriate, and no follow-up is necessary.
